191	    provider = algorithm.providers.model
192	    draft_config, draft_model = _load_draft(cfg, algorithm)
193	    needs_input_tools = provider.needs_input_tools(cfg, draft_model)
194	    input_tools = _load_input_tools(cfg, algorithm) if needs_input_tools else None
195	    target_config = load_target_config(
196	        cfg.model.target_model_path,
197	        cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
198	        trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
199	    )
200	    text_config = target_text_config(target_config)
201	    target_hidden_size = int(text_config.hidden_size)
202	    target_vocab_size = resolve_target_vocab_size(target_config)
203	    draft_vocab_size = int(
204	        getattr(draft_config, "draft_vocab_size", draft_config.vocab_size)
205	    )
206	
207	    parts = provider.build_training_model(
208	        cfg, draft_model, draft_config, target_config, input_tools
209	    )
210	    if cfg.mode == "online" and parts.capture_layers is None:
211	        parts.capture_layers = provider.resolve_capture_layers(
212	            cfg, draft_config, target_config
213	        )
214	
215	    # Keep the composite and target parts bf16 while avoiding accidental target
216	    # gradients. The optimizer still receives the strategy's trainable module.
217	    if parts.target_head is not None and isinstance(parts.target_head, torch.nn.Module):
218	        parts.target_head.requires_grad_(False)
219	
220	    return ModelBundle(
221	        model=parts.model,
222	        draft_model=draft_model,
223	        draft_config=draft_config,
224	        input_tools=input_tools,
225	        target_head=parts.target_head,
226	        target_hidden_size=target_hidden_size,
227	        target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
228	        draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
229	        capture_layers=parts.capture_layers,
230	        # This mapping also carries the provider-bound checkpoint policy. It is
231	        # forwarded unchanged through every trainer-bearing topology.
232	        strategy_kwargs=algorithm.providers.step.bind_runtime(
233	            cfg,
234	            draft_model,
235	            parts.model,
236	            model_provenance=_model_resume_provenance(
237	                cfg,
238	                draft_config,
239	                target_config,
240	                capture_layers=parts.capture_layers,
241	            ),
242	        ),
243	    )

246	class _ConfiguredOptimizerFactory:
247	    def __init__(self, cfg: Config) -> None:
248	        self.cfg = cfg
249	        self.total_steps = cfg.training.total_steps or cfg.training.max_steps
250	
251	    def configure_total_steps(self, total_steps: int) -> None:
252	        if self.total_steps is None:
253	            self.total_steps = total_steps
254	        elif self.total_steps != total_steps:
255	            raise ValueError(
256	                "optimizer/controller schedule mismatch: "
257	                f"{self.total_steps} != {total_steps}"
258	            )
259	
260	    def __call__(self, draft_module):
261	        from specforge.optimizer import BF16Optimizer
262	
263	        if self.total_steps is None:
264	            raise RuntimeError("optimizer total_steps was not resolved before assembly")
265	        t = self.cfg.training
266	        return BF16Optimizer(
267	            draft_module,
268	            lr=t.learning_rate,
269	            max_grad_norm=t.max_grad_norm,
270	            warmup_ratio=t.warmup_ratio,
271	            total_steps=self.total_steps,
272	            offload_master=t.optimizer_cpu_offload,
273	        )

280	def _logger(metrics, step):
281	    printable = {}
282	    for key, value in metrics.items():
283	        try:
284	            printable[key] = float(value)
285	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
286	            try:
287	                printable[key] = [float(item) for item in value]
288	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
289	                continue
290	    print(f"step {step}: {printable}", flush=True)

293	def _configured_logger(cfg: Config):
294	    """Create an external tracker only for a trainer-bearing run."""
295	    if cfg.tracking.report_to == "none" or cfg.training.role == "producer":
296	        return _logger
297	
298	    from types import SimpleNamespace
299	
300	    from specforge.training.tracking import create_tracker_logger
301	
302	    options = cfg.tracking.model_dump()
303	    options["wandb_project"] = options["wandb_project"] or "specforge"
304	    options["wandb_name"] = options["wandb_name"] or cfg.run_id
305	    options["swanlab_project"] = options["swanlab_project"] or "specforge"
306	    options["swanlab_name"] = options["swanlab_name"] or cfg.run_id
307	    options["mlflow_experiment_name"] = options["mlflow_experiment_name"] or "specforge"
308	    options["mlflow_run_name"] = options["mlflow_run_name"] or cfg.run_id
309	    return create_tracker_logger(
310	        SimpleNamespace(**options), cfg.output_dir, console_logger=_logger
311	    )

314	def _close_configured_logger(logger) -> None:
315	    close = getattr(logger, "close", None)
316	    if callable(close):

320	def _prompt_cache_key(cfg: Config, *, path: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
321	    source_path = path or cfg.data.prompts_path or cfg.data.train_data_path
322	    content_hash = None
323	    if source_path and os.path.isfile(source_path):
324	        source_hasher = hashlib.sha256()
325	        with open(source_path, "rb") as source_file:
326	            for chunk in iter(lambda: source_file.read(8 * 1024 * 1024), b""):
327	                source_hasher.update(chunk)
328	        content_hash = source_hasher.hexdigest()
329	
330	    identity = {
331	        "path": source_path,
332	        "content_hash": content_hash,
333	        "max_length": cfg.data.max_length,
334	        "chat_template": cfg.data.chat_template,
335	        "is_preformatted": cfg.data.is_preformatted,
336	        "train_only_last_turn": cfg.data.train_only_last_turn,
337	        "max_prompts": cfg.data.max_prompts,
338	        "target_model": cfg.model.target_model_path,
339	        "draft_config": cfg.model.draft_model_config,
340	        "draft_checkpoint": cfg.model.draft_checkpoint_path,
341	        "draft_num_hidden_layers": cfg.model.draft_num_hidden_layers,
342	        "draft_block_size": cfg.model.draft_block_size,
343	        "strategy": cfg.training.strategy,
344	        "input_modality": cfg.model.input_modality,
345	    }
346	    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(identity, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest()
347	
348	
349	def _prepare_prompts(
350	    cfg: Config,
351	    tokenizer,
352	    *,
353	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
354	    draft_config,
355	    path: Optional[str] = None,
356	    cache_key: Optional[str] = None,
357	) -> List[dict]:
358	    """Prepare one prompt source with an optional path/cache namespace override.
359	
360	    Training keeps the configured cache key. Evaluation supplies its own path
361	    and derived key so it can never read or overwrite the training prompt cache.
362	    """
363	    if cfg.model.input_modality != "text":
364	        raise ValueError(
365	            "the built-in prompt preparer supports only modality 'text'; "
366	            f"algorithm {algorithm.name!r} must provide a ServerInputAdapter "
367	            f"for {cfg.model.input_modality!r}"
368	        )
369	    from specforge.data.prompt_builder import prepare_prompt_tasks
370	
371	    configured_path = cfg.data.prompts_path or cfg.data.train_data_path
372	    source_path = path or configured_path
373	    if not source_path:
374	        raise ValueError("prompt preparation requires a non-empty data path")
375	    if cache_key is None:
376	        cache_key = (
377	            cfg.data.cache_key
378	            if path is None and cfg.data.cache_key is not None
379	            else _prompt_cache_key(cfg, path=source_path)
380	        )
381	    min_loss_tokens = algorithm.providers.model.minimum_loss_tokens(cfg, draft_config)
382	    return prepare_prompt_tasks(
383	        source_path,
384	        tokenizer,
385	        chat_template=cfg.data.chat_template,
386	        max_length=cfg.data.max_length,
387	        is_preformatted=cfg.data.is_preformatted,
388	        train_only_last_turn=cfg.data.train_only_last_turn,
389	        cache_dir=cfg.data.cache_dir,
390	        cache_key=cache_key,
391	        num_proc=cfg.data.build_dataset_num_proc,
392	        min_loss_tokens=min_loss_tokens,
393	        max_prompts=cfg.data.max_prompts,
394	    )
395	
396	
397	def _install_dataset_vocab_mapping(
398	    cfg: Config,
399	    bundle: ModelBundle,
400	    *,
401	    counts: Counter,
402	    dataset_identity: str,
403	) -> None:
404	    """Build, cache, and install one deterministic EAGLE vocabulary map."""
405	    if (
406	        cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path
407	        or bundle.draft_vocab_size == bundle.target_vocab_size
408	    ):
409	        return
410	
411	    key = hashlib.sha256(
412	        (
413	            f"{dataset_identity}:{bundle.target_vocab_size}:"
414	            f"{bundle.draft_vocab_size}"
415	        ).encode()
416	    ).hexdigest()
417	    directory = os.path.join(cfg.data.cache_dir, "vocab_mapping")
418	    os.makedirs(directory, exist_ok=True)
419	    path = os.path.join(directory, f"{key}.pt")
420	    if os.path.exists(path):
421	        bundle.draft_model.load_vocab_mapping(path)
422	        return
423	
424	    import torch
425	
426	    from specforge.data.preprocessing import process_token_dict_to_mappings
427	
428	    d2t, t2d = process_token_dict_to_mappings(
429	        counts,
430	        bundle.draft_vocab_size,
431	        bundle.target_vocab_size,
432	    )
433	
434	    # Every rank derives the same mapping and installs it directly, so training
435	    # does not depend on a shared cache filesystem. Rank 0 alone persists the
436	    # reusable cache file, avoiding concurrent torch.save writes.
437	    bundle.draft_model.d2t.copy_(d2t)
438	    bundle.draft_model.t2d.copy_(t2d)
439	    bundle.draft_model.vocab_mapping_loaded = True
440	    distributed = (
441	        torch.distributed.is_available() and torch.distributed.is_initialized()
442	    )
443	    if not distributed or torch.distributed.get_rank() == 0:
444	        temporary = f"{path}.{os.getpid()}.tmp"
445	        torch.save({"d2t": d2t, "t2d": t2d}, temporary)
446	        os.replace(temporary, path)
447	
448	
449	def _ensure_offline_vocab_mapping(
450	    cfg: Config,
451	    bundle: ModelBundle,
452	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
453	) -> None:
454	    """Derive a local-offline map from the exact feature ids and loss masks."""
455	    if FeatureMode.OFFLINE not in algorithm.providers.vocab_mapping_modes:
456	        return
457	    if (
458	        cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path
459	        or bundle.draft_vocab_size == bundle.target_vocab_size
460	    ):
461	        return
462	
463	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import list_feature_files
464	    from specforge.training.vocab_mapping import count_effective_feature_tokens
465	
466	    identity_parts = []
467	    for path in list_feature_files(cfg.data.hidden_states_path):
468	        stat = os.stat(path)
469	        identity_parts.append((os.path.abspath(path), stat.st_size, stat.st_mtime_ns))
470	    identity = json.dumps(
471	        {
472	            "kind": "offline-features-v1",
473	            "files": identity_parts,
474	            "max_length": cfg.data.max_length,
475	        },
476	        sort_keys=True,
477	    )
478	    counts = count_effective_feature_tokens(
479	        cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
480	        max_length=cfg.data.max_length,
481	        target_vocab_size=bundle.target_vocab_size,
482	    )
483	    _install_dataset_vocab_mapping(
484	        cfg,
485	        bundle,
486	        counts=counts,
487	        dataset_identity=identity,
488	    )
489	
490	
491	def _dataloader_num_workers(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration) -> int:
492	    dataloader_num_workers = cfg.data.dataloader_num_workers
493	    if dataloader_num_workers is None:
494	        dataloader_num_workers = (
495	            algorithm.providers.model.default_dataloader_num_workers
496	        )
497	    return dataloader_num_workers
498	
499	
500	def _profiling_options(cfg: Config):
501	    from specforge.training.profiling import ProfilingOptions
502	
503	    return ProfilingOptions(
504	        enabled=cfg.profiling.enabled,
505	        start_step=cfg.profiling.start_step,
506	        num_steps=cfg.profiling.num_steps,
507	        record_shapes=cfg.profiling.record_shapes,
508	    )
509	
510	
511	def _common_launch_kwargs(
512	    cfg: Config,
513	    bundle: ModelBundle,
514	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
515	    *,
516	    logger=_logger,
517	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
518	    t = cfg.training
519	    # USP shards one logical sample over ``sp_size`` ranks.  Preserve the
520	    # legacy optimizer-window semantics: one user accumulation unit represents
521	    # a complete logical sequence, not one local sequence shard.
522	    accumulation_steps = t.accumulation_steps
523	    if t.attention_backend == "usp":
524	        accumulation_steps *= t.sp_ulysses_size * t.sp_ring_size
525	    return dict(
526	        algorithm=algorithm,
527	        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
528	        optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory(cfg),
529	        run_id=cfg.run_id,
530	        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
531	        batch_size=t.batch_size,
532	        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
533	        max_steps=t.max_steps,
534	        total_steps=t.total_steps,
535	        save_interval=t.save_interval,
536	        eval_interval=t.eval_interval,
537	        max_checkpoints=t.max_checkpoints,
538	        logger=logger,
539	        log_interval=t.log_interval,
540	        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
541	        tp_size=t.tp_size,
542	        sp_ulysses_size=t.sp_ulysses_size,
543	        sp_ring_size=t.sp_ring_size,
544	        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
545	        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
546	    )
547	
548	
549	def build_training_run(
550	    cfg: Config,
551	    *,
552	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
553	) -> TrainingRun:
554	    """Assemble one validated run from an already-resolved algorithm.
555	
556	    Offline training may run in one process or with a disaggregated feature
557	    source.  Online training is always disaggregated and captures through
558	    external SGLang servers; colocated online execution is intentionally absent.
559	    """
560	
561	    if algorithm.name != cfg.training.strategy:
562	        raise ValueError(
563	            "resolved algorithm does not match training.strategy: "
564	            f"{algorithm.name!r} != {cfg.training.strategy!r}"
565	        )
566	
567	    t = cfg.training
568	    if t.role != "producer":
569	        import torch.distributed as dist
570	
571	        cfg.validate_world_size(dist.get_world_size() if dist.is_initialized() else 1)
572	
573	    if cfg.mode == "online" and cfg.deployment.mode != "disaggregated":
574	        raise ValueError(
575	            "online training is server-only and requires "
576	            "deployment.mode='disaggregated'"
577	        )
578	
579	    if cfg.deployment.mode == "disaggregated":
580	        from specforge.training.disaggregated import build_disaggregated_run
581	
582	        run_logger = _configured_logger(cfg)
583	        try:
584	            return build_disaggregated_run(
585	                cfg,
586	                algorithm=algorithm,
587	                build_model_bundle=lambda run_cfg: build_model_bundle(
588	                    run_cfg, algorithm=algorithm
589	                ),
590	                prepare_prompts=lambda run_cfg, tokenizer, **kwargs: _prepare_prompts(
591	                    run_cfg,
592	                    tokenizer,
593	                    algorithm=algorithm,
594	                    **kwargs,
595	                ),
596	                optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory,
597	                logger=run_logger,
598	            )
599	        except BaseException:
600	            _close_configured_logger(run_logger)
601	            raise
602	
603	    if cfg.mode != "offline":
604	        raise ValueError("colocated execution supports offline training only")
605	
606	    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg, algorithm=algorithm)
607	    from specforge.launch import build_offline_runtime
608	
609	    _ensure_offline_vocab_mapping(cfg, bundle, algorithm)
610	    run_logger = _configured_logger(cfg)
611	    try:
612	        trainer = build_offline_runtime(
613	            hidden_states_path=cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
614	            eval_hidden_states_path=cfg.data.eval_hidden_states_path or None,
615	            draft_model=bundle.model,
616	            target_head=bundle.target_head,
617	            ttt_length=t.ttt_length,
618	            max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
619	            num_epochs=t.num_epochs,
620	            use_usp_preprocess=(t.attention_backend == "usp"),
621	            seed=t.seed,
622	            resume_from=t.resume_from,
623	            **_common_launch_kwargs(
624	                cfg,
625	                bundle,
626	                algorithm,
627	                logger=run_logger,
628	            ),
629	        )
630	    except BaseException:
631	        _close_configured_logger(run_logger)
632	        raise
633	    return TrainingRun(trainer=trainer)
634	
635	
636	__all__ = [
637	    "ModelBundle",
638	    "TrainingRun",
639	    "build_model_bundle",
640	    "build_training_run",
641	]
642

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
"""Training-object assembly driven by the application composition root.

The application resolves one immutable algorithm registration and passes it
through every builder.  This module never resolves an algorithm name and never
constructs an in-process online target engine; online capture comes exclusively
from an external SGLang server through the disaggregated runtime.

Heavy model/data dependencies stay lazy so importing :mod:`specforge.training`
does not load Transformers, datasets, or a target backend.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import os
from collections import Counter
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Mapping, Optional

from specforge.algorithms.contracts import FeatureMode
from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
from specforge.config import Config
from specforge.training.provenance import (
    model_resume_provenance as _model_resume_provenance,
)


@dataclass
class ModelBundle:
    """Objects and capture metadata needed by one configured training run."""

    model: Any
    draft_model: Any
    draft_config: Any
    input_tools: Any = None
    target_head: Any = None
    target_hidden_size: int = 0
    target_vocab_size: int = 0
    draft_vocab_size: int = 0
    capture_layers: Optional[List[int]] = None
    strategy_kwargs: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class TrainingRun:
    """A fully assembled run with one lifecycle for rollout and training."""

    trainer: Any = None
    execute: Optional[Callable[[], int]] = None
    on_success: Optional[Callable[[int], None]] = None
    on_failure: Optional[Callable[[BaseException], None]] = None
    on_finally: Optional[Callable[[], None]] = None

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        if (self.trainer is None) == (self.execute is None):
            raise ValueError("a training run needs exactly one trainer or executor")
        if self.execute is not None and any(
            hook is not None
            for hook in (self.on_success, self.on_failure, self.on_finally)
        ):
            raise ValueError("lifecycle hooks belong to trainer-bearing runs only")

    def run(self) -> int:
        if self.execute is not None:
            return self.execute()
        try:
            result = self.trainer.fit()
            if self.on_success is not None:
                self.on_success(result)
            return result
        except BaseException as exc:
            if self.on_failure is not None:
                self.on_failure(exc)
            raise
        finally:
            if self.on_finally is not None:
                self.on_finally()


def _target_text_config(config):
    return getattr(config, "text_config", config)


def _torch_dtype(name: str):
    import torch

    return getattr(torch, name)


def _device():
    from specforge.utils import get_local_device

    return get_local_device()


def _load_draft(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration):
    """Construct the configured draft model without any legacy trainer code."""
    from specforge.modeling.draft.registry import resolve_draft
    from specforge.training.model_loading import resolve_draft_config

    provider = algorithm.providers.model
    draft_config = resolve_draft_config(cfg, provider=provider.draft_config)
    draft_model = provider.build_draft(cfg, draft_config)
    architecture = provider.draft_config.architecture
    expected_type = resolve_draft(architecture)
    if not isinstance(draft_model, expected_type):
        raise ValueError(
            f"training.strategy={algorithm.name!r} requires {architecture}, but "
            f"the resolved draft config builds "
            f"{type(draft_model).__name__}"
        )
    return draft_config, draft_model


def _load_text_tokenizer(cfg: Config):
    """Load tokenizer tooling used by current built-in text providers."""
    if cfg.model.input_modality != "text":
        raise ValueError(
            "built-in algorithms currently provide training-model input tooling "
            f"only for modality 'text', got {cfg.model.input_modality!r}; "
            "another modality must add its own input provider"
        )
    from transformers import AutoTokenizer

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(
        cfg.model.target_model_path,
        cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
        trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
    )
    if cfg.model.tokenizer_pad_token_id is not None:
        tokenizer.pad_token_id = cfg.model.tokenizer_pad_token_id
    elif tokenizer.pad_token_id is None:
        fallback_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
        if isinstance(fallback_id, (list, tuple)):
            fallback_id = fallback_id[0] if fallback_id else None

        if fallback_id is None:
            fallback_id = tokenizer.unk_token_id
        if fallback_id is None:
            raise ValueError(
                "target tokenizer has no pad, EOS, or unknown token ID; set "
                "model.tokenizer_pad_token_id explicitly"
            )
        tokenizer.pad_token_id = fallback_id
    return tokenizer


def _load_input_tools(
    cfg: Config,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    *,
    input_adapter=None,
):
    """Load modality tooling through the provider port or the text default."""

    if input_adapter is None and cfg.mode == "online":
        streaming = algorithm.providers.server_streaming_for(cfg.model.input_modality)
        input_adapter = streaming.create_input_adapter(cfg)
    if input_adapter is not None:
        return input_adapter.load_input_tools(cfg)
    return _load_text_tokenizer(cfg)


def build_model_bundle(cfg: Config, *, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration) -> ModelBundle:
    """Build the method-specific composite model and frozen target pieces."""
    import torch

    from specforge.modeling.target.target_utils import (
        load_target_config,
        target_text_config,
    )
    from specforge.modeling.target.target_utils import (
        target_vocab_size as resolve_target_vocab_size,
    )

    provider = algorithm.providers.model
    draft_config, draft_model = _load_draft(cfg, algorithm)
    needs_input_tools = provider.needs_input_tools(cfg, draft_model)
    input_tools = _load_input_tools(cfg, algorithm) if needs_input_tools else None
    target_config = load_target_config(
        cfg.model.target_model_path,
        cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
        trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
    )
    text_config = target_text_config(target_config)
    target_hidden_size = int(text_config.hidden_size)
    target_vocab_size = resolve_target_vocab_size(target_config)
    draft_vocab_size = int(
        getattr(draft_config, "draft_vocab_size", draft_config.vocab_size)
    )

    parts = provider.build_training_model(
        cfg, draft_model, draft_config, target_config, input_tools
    )
    if cfg.mode == "online" and parts.capture_layers is None:
        parts.capture_layers = provider.resolve_capture_layers(
            cfg, draft_config, target_config
        )

    # Keep the composite and target parts bf16 while avoiding accidental target
    # gradients. The optimizer still receives the strategy's trainable module.
    if parts.target_head is not None and isinstance(parts.target_head, torch.nn.Module):
        parts.target_head.requires_grad_(False)

    return ModelBundle(
        model=parts.model,
        draft_model=draft_model,
        draft_config=draft_config,
        input_tools=input_tools,
        target_head=parts.target_head,
        target_hidden_size=target_hidden_size,
        target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
        draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
        capture_layers=parts.capture_layers,
        # This mapping also carries the provider-bound checkpoint policy. It is
        # forwarded unchanged through every trainer-bearing topology.
        strategy_kwargs=algorithm.providers.step.bind_runtime(
            cfg,
            draft_model,
            parts.model,
            model_provenance=_model_resume_provenance(
                cfg,
                draft_config,
                target_config,
                capture_layers=parts.capture_layers,
            ),
        ),
    )


class _ConfiguredOptimizerFactory:
    def __init__(self, cfg: Config) -> None:
        self.cfg = cfg
        self.total_steps = cfg.training.total_steps or cfg.training.max_steps

    def configure_total_steps(self, total_steps: int) -> None:
        if self.total_steps is None:
            self.total_steps = total_steps
        elif self.total_steps != total_steps:
            raise ValueError(
                "optimizer/controller schedule mismatch: "
                f"{self.total_steps} != {total_steps}"
            )

    def __call__(self, draft_module):
        from specforge.optimizer import BF16Optimizer

        if self.total_steps is None:
            raise RuntimeError("optimizer total_steps was not resolved before assembly")
        t = self.cfg.training
        return BF16Optimizer(
            draft_module,
            lr=t.learning_rate,
            max_grad_norm=t.max_grad_norm,
            warmup_ratio=t.warmup_ratio,
            total_steps=self.total_steps,
            offload_master=t.optimizer_cpu_offload,
        )


def _optimizer_factory(cfg: Config):
    return _ConfiguredOptimizerFactory(cfg)


def _logger(metrics, step):
    printable = {}
    for key, value in metrics.items():
        try:
            printable[key] = float(value)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            try:
                printable[key] = [float(item) for item in value]
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                continue
    print(f"step {step}: {printable}", flush=True)


def _configured_logger(cfg: Config):
    """Create an external tracker only for a trainer-bearing run."""
    if cfg.tracking.report_to == "none" or cfg.training.role == "producer":
        return _logger

    from types import SimpleNamespace

    from specforge.training.tracking import create_tracker_logger

    options = cfg.tracking.model_dump()
    options["wandb_project"] = options["wandb_project"] or "specforge"
    options["wandb_name"] = options["wandb_name"] or cfg.run_id
    options["swanlab_project"] = options["swanlab_project"] or "specforge"
    options["swanlab_name"] = options["swanlab_name"] or cfg.run_id
    options["mlflow_experiment_name"] = options["mlflow_experiment_name"] or "specforge"
    options["mlflow_run_name"] = options["mlflow_run_name"] or cfg.run_id
    return create_tracker_logger(
        SimpleNamespace(**options), cfg.output_dir, console_logger=_logger
    )


def _close_configured_logger(logger) -> None:
    close = getattr(logger, "close", None)
    if callable(close):
        close()


def _prompt_cache_key(cfg: Config, *, path: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
    source_path = path or cfg.data.prompts_path or cfg.data.train_data_path
    content_hash = None
    if source_path and os.path.isfile(source_path):
        source_hasher = hashlib.sha256()
        with open(source_path, "rb") as source_file:
            for chunk in iter(lambda: source_file.read(8 * 1024 * 1024), b""):
                source_hasher.update(chunk)
        content_hash = source_hasher.hexdigest()

    identity = {
        "path": source_path,
        "content_hash": content_hash,
        "max_length": cfg.data.max_length,
        "chat_template": cfg.data.chat_template,
        "is_preformatted": cfg.data.is_preformatted,
        "train_only_last_turn": cfg.data.train_only_last_turn,
        "max_prompts": cfg.data.max_prompts,
        "target_model": cfg.model.target_model_path,
        "draft_config": cfg.model.draft_model_config,
        "draft_checkpoint": cfg.model.draft_checkpoint_path,
        "draft_num_hidden_layers": cfg.model.draft_num_hidden_layers,
        "draft_block_size": cfg.model.draft_block_size,
        "strategy": cfg.training.strategy,
        "input_modality": cfg.model.input_modality,
    }
    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(identity, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest()


def _prepare_prompts(
    cfg: Config,
    tokenizer,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    draft_config,
    path: Optional[str] = None,
    cache_key: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[dict]:
    """Prepare one prompt source with an optional path/cache namespace override.

    Training keeps the configured cache key. Evaluation supplies its own path
    and derived key so it can never read or overwrite the training prompt cache.
    """
    if cfg.model.input_modality != "text":
        raise ValueError(
            "the built-in prompt preparer supports only modality 'text'; "
            f"algorithm {algorithm.name!r} must provide a ServerInputAdapter "
            f"for {cfg.model.input_modality!r}"
        )
    from specforge.data.prompt_builder import prepare_prompt_tasks

    configured_path = cfg.data.prompts_path or cfg.data.train_data_path
    source_path = path or configured_path
    if not source_path:
        raise ValueError("prompt preparation requires a non-empty data path")
    if cache_key is None:
        cache_key = (
            cfg.data.cache_key
            if path is None and cfg.data.cache_key is not None
            else _prompt_cache_key(cfg, path=source_path)
        )
    min_loss_tokens = algorithm.providers.model.minimum_loss_tokens(cfg, draft_config)
    return prepare_prompt_tasks(
        source_path,
        tokenizer,
        chat_template=cfg.data.chat_template,
        max_length=cfg.data.max_length,
        is_preformatted=cfg.data.is_preformatted,
        train_only_last_turn=cfg.data.train_only_last_turn,
        cache_dir=cfg.data.cache_dir,
        cache_key=cache_key,
        num_proc=cfg.data.build_dataset_num_proc,
        min_loss_tokens=min_loss_tokens,
        max_prompts=cfg.data.max_prompts,
    )


def _install_dataset_vocab_mapping(
    cfg: Config,
    bundle: ModelBundle,
    *,
    counts: Counter,
    dataset_identity: str,
) -> None:
    """Build, cache, and install one deterministic EAGLE vocabulary map."""
    if (
        cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path
        or bundle.draft_vocab_size == bundle.target_vocab_size
    ):
        return

    key = hashlib.sha256(
        (
            f"{dataset_identity}:{bundle.target_vocab_size}:"
            f"{bundle.draft_vocab_size}"
        ).encode()
    ).hexdigest()
    directory = os.path.join(cfg.data.cache_dir, "vocab_mapping")
    os.makedirs(directory, exist_ok=True)
    path = os.path.join(directory, f"{key}.pt")
    if os.path.exists(path):
        bundle.draft_model.load_vocab_mapping(path)
        return

    import torch

    from specforge.data.preprocessing import process_token_dict_to_mappings

    d2t, t2d = process_token_dict_to_mappings(
        counts,
        bundle.draft_vocab_size,
        bundle.target_vocab_size,
    )

    # Every rank derives the same mapping and installs it directly, so training
    # does not depend on a shared cache filesystem. Rank 0 alone persists the
    # reusable cache file, avoiding concurrent torch.save writes.
    bundle.draft_model.d2t.copy_(d2t)
    bundle.draft_model.t2d.copy_(t2d)
    bundle.draft_model.vocab_mapping_loaded = True
    distributed = (
        torch.distributed.is_available() and torch.distributed.is_initialized()
    )
    if not distributed or torch.distributed.get_rank() == 0:
        temporary = f"{path}.{os.getpid()}.tmp"
        torch.save({"d2t": d2t, "t2d": t2d}, temporary)
        os.replace(temporary, path)


def _ensure_offline_vocab_mapping(
    cfg: Config,
    bundle: ModelBundle,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
) -> None:
    """Derive a local-offline map from the exact feature ids and loss masks."""
    if FeatureMode.OFFLINE not in algorithm.providers.vocab_mapping_modes:
        return
    if (
        cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path
        or bundle.draft_vocab_size == bundle.target_vocab_size
    ):
        return

    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import list_feature_files
    from specforge.training.vocab_mapping import count_effective_feature_tokens

    identity_parts = []
    for path in list_feature_files(cfg.data.hidden_states_path):
        stat = os.stat(path)
        identity_parts.append((os.path.abspath(path), stat.st_size, stat.st_mtime_ns))
    identity = json.dumps(
        {
            "kind": "offline-features-v1",
            "files": identity_parts,
            "max_length": cfg.data.max_length,
        },
        sort_keys=True,
    )
    counts = count_effective_feature_tokens(
        cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
        max_length=cfg.data.max_length,
        target_vocab_size=bundle.target_vocab_size,
    )
    _install_dataset_vocab_mapping(
        cfg,
        bundle,
        counts=counts,
        dataset_identity=identity,
    )


def _dataloader_num_workers(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration) -> int:
    dataloader_num_workers = cfg.data.dataloader_num_workers
    if dataloader_num_workers is None:
        dataloader_num_workers = (
            algorithm.providers.model.default_dataloader_num_workers
        )
    return dataloader_num_workers


def _profiling_options(cfg: Config):
    from specforge.training.profiling import ProfilingOptions

    return ProfilingOptions(
        enabled=cfg.profiling.enabled,
        start_step=cfg.profiling.start_step,
        num_steps=cfg.profiling.num_steps,
        record_shapes=cfg.profiling.record_shapes,
    )


def _common_launch_kwargs(
    cfg: Config,
    bundle: ModelBundle,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    *,
    logger=_logger,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    t = cfg.training
    # USP shards one logical sample over ``sp_size`` ranks.  Preserve the
    # legacy optimizer-window semantics: one user accumulation unit represents
    # a complete logical sequence, not one local sequence shard.
    accumulation_steps = t.accumulation_steps
    if t.attention_backend == "usp":
        accumulation_steps *= t.sp_ulysses_size * t.sp_ring_size
    return dict(
        algorithm=algorithm,
        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
        optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory(cfg),
        run_id=cfg.run_id,
        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
        batch_size=t.batch_size,
        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
        max_steps=t.max_steps,
        total_steps=t.total_steps,
        save_interval=t.save_interval,
        eval_interval=t.eval_interval,
        max_checkpoints=t.max_checkpoints,
        logger=logger,
        log_interval=t.log_interval,
        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
        tp_size=t.tp_size,
        sp_ulysses_size=t.sp_ulysses_size,
        sp_ring_size=t.sp_ring_size,
        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
    )


def build_training_run(
    cfg: Config,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
) -> TrainingRun:
    """Assemble one validated run from an already-resolved algorithm.

    Offline training may run in one process or with a disaggregated feature
    source.  Online training is always disaggregated and captures through
    external SGLang servers; colocated online execution is intentionally absent.
    """

    if algorithm.name != cfg.training.strategy:
        raise ValueError(
            "resolved algorithm does not match training.strategy: "
            f"{algorithm.name!r} != {cfg.training.strategy!r}"
        )

    t = cfg.training
    if t.role != "producer":
        import torch.distributed as dist

        cfg.validate_world_size(dist.get_world_size() if dist.is_initialized() else 1)

    if cfg.mode == "online" and cfg.deployment.mode != "disaggregated":
        raise ValueError(
            "online training is server-only and requires "
            "deployment.mode='disaggregated'"
        )

    if cfg.deployment.mode == "disaggregated":
        from specforge.training.disaggregated import build_disaggregated_run

        run_logger = _configured_logger(cfg)
        try:
            return build_disaggregated_run(
                cfg,
                algorithm=algorithm,
                build_model_bundle=lambda run_cfg: build_model_bundle(
                    run_cfg, algorithm=algorithm
                ),
                prepare_prompts=lambda run_cfg, tokenizer, **kwargs: _prepare_prompts(
                    run_cfg,
                    tokenizer,
                    algorithm=algorithm,
                    **kwargs,
                ),
                optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory,
                logger=run_logger,
            )
        except BaseException:
            _close_configured_logger(run_logger)
            raise

    if cfg.mode != "offline":
        raise ValueError("colocated execution supports offline training only")

    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg, algorithm=algorithm)
    from specforge.launch import build_offline_runtime

    _ensure_offline_vocab_mapping(cfg, bundle, algorithm)
    run_logger = _configured_logger(cfg)
    try:
        trainer = build_offline_runtime(
            hidden_states_path=cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
            eval_hidden_states_path=cfg.data.eval_hidden_states_path or None,
            draft_model=bundle.model,
            target_head=bundle.target_head,
            ttt_length=t.ttt_length,
            max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
            num_epochs=t.num_epochs,
            use_usp_preprocess=(t.attention_backend == "usp"),
            seed=t.seed,
            resume_from=t.resume_from,
            **_common_launch_kwargs(
                cfg,
                bundle,
                algorithm,
                logger=run_logger,
            ),
        )
    except BaseException:
        _close_configured_logger(run_logger)
        raise
    return TrainingRun(trainer=trainer)


__all__ = [
    "ModelBundle",
    "TrainingRun",
    "build_model_bundle",
    "build_training_run",
]

aiTitle: SpecForge 拓扑构建器 launch 讲义生成

content.thinking:
Now I understand the dispatch chain much better:

1. `cli.py` → `build_application_run(resolved).run()` → 
2. `composition.py:build_application_run` → `build_training_run` in `assembly.py` →
3. `assembly.py:build_training_run`:
   - If `deployment.mode == "disaggregated"` → `build_disaggregated_run` in `training/disaggregated.py`
   - Else if `mode == "offline"` → `build_offline_runtime` in `launch.py` (colocated offline)
   - Else raise error (colocated online unsupported)

So the four topology builders are actually called from:
- `assembly.py` calls `build_offline_runtime` directly (colocated offline path)
- `disaggregated.py` calls the other three (`build_disagg_offline_runtime`, `build_disagg_online_producer`, `build_disagg_online_consumer`)

Let me read `disaggregated.py` to understand how it dispatches to the three disagg builders. This is important for the lecture.

9	"""Disaggregated run assembly used by ``specforge train``.
10	
11	The role is configuration, not a different Python entry point. Producer and
12	consumer processes load the same run file with ``training.role`` set to their
13	respective role. Cross-process transport values intentionally remain environment
14	variables because they are deployment secrets/addresses rather than model
15	hyperparameters.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import json
21	import os
22	import time
23	from typing import Callable, Optional, Sequence
24	
25	from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
26	from specforge.config import Config
27	
28	_PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX = ".producer_claim"
29	_ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX = ".schedule.json"
30	_ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (
31	    ".closed",
32	    ".consumed_count",
33	    ".failed",
34	    ".consumer_done",
35	    ".consumer_failed",
36	    ".consumer_quantum",
37	    _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
38	)
39	_OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (".done", ".consumed", ".failed", ".consumer_failed")
40	
41	
42	def _write_control(path: str, value: str = "") -> None:
43	    """Atomically publish one small filesystem control record."""
44	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
45	    tmp = f"{path}.tmp.{os.getpid()}"
46	    with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
47	        stream.write(value)
48	        stream.flush()
49	        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
50	    os.replace(tmp, path)
51	
52	
53	def _read_control(path: str) -> Optional[str]:
54	    try:
55	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
56	            return stream.read().strip() or "unknown remote failure"
57	    except FileNotFoundError:
58	        return None
59	
60	
61	def _publish_control_failure(path: str, exc: BaseException) -> None:
62	    """Best-effort peer notification that never hides the root exception."""
63	    try:
64	        _write_control(path, f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
65	    except Exception as signal_exc:
66	        print(
67	            f"failed to publish role failure to {path}: {signal_exc}",
68	            flush=True,
69	        )
70	
71	
72	def _claim_fresh_control_path(path: str, suffixes: Sequence[str]) -> None:
73	    """Reject artifact reuse and atomically claim a new producer attempt."""
74	    claim = path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX
75	    artifacts = [path, claim, *(path + suffix for suffix in suffixes)]
76	    existing = [item for item in artifacts if os.path.exists(item)]
77	    if existing:
78	        raise ValueError(
79	            f"control path {path!r} has artifacts from an existing attempt: "
80	            f"{existing}; choose a new attempt-specific path"
81	        )
82	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
83	    try:
84	        fd = os.open(claim, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
85	    except FileExistsError as exc:
86	        raise ValueError(
87	            f"control path {path!r} is already claimed by another producer; "
88	            "choose a new attempt-specific path"
89	        ) from exc
90	    with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
91	        stream.write(f"pid={os.getpid()}\n")
92	        stream.flush()
93	        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
94	
95	
96	def _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg: Config, exc: BaseException) -> None:
97	    """Notify the peer when a role fails before its run closure is returned.
98	
99	    Producer notification is allowed only after this process successfully
100	    claimed the attempt path.  That prevents a typo/retry using an occupied
101	    path from poisoning another live producer's channel.
102	    """
103	    env_name = "DISAGG_MANIFEST" if cfg.mode == "offline" else "DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL"
104	    path = os.environ.get(env_name)
105	    if not path:
106	        return
107	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
108	        claim = _read_control(path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX)
109	        if claim != f"pid={os.getpid()}":
110	            return
111	        failure_path = path + ".failed"
112	    else:
113	        failure_path = path + ".consumer_failed"
114	    _publish_control_failure(failure_path, exc)
115	
116	
117	def _primary_rank() -> bool:
118	    return int(os.environ.get("RANK", "0")) == 0
119	
120	
121	def _env(name: str) -> str:
122	    value = os.environ.get(name)
123	    if not value:
124	        raise ValueError(f"disaggregated training requires environment variable {name}")
125	    return value
126	
127	
128	def _mooncake_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
129	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import AuthPolicy
130	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.mooncake_store import MooncakeFeatureStore
131	
132	    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
133	    setup_kwargs = {
134	        "local_hostname": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_HOSTNAME", "127.0.0.1"),
135	        "metadata_server": _env("MOONCAKE_METADATA_SERVER"),
136	        "master_server_addr": _env("MOONCAKE_MASTER_SERVER_ADDR"),
137	        "protocol": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_PROTOCOL", "tcp"),
138	        "rdma_devices": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_RDMA_DEVICES", ""),
139	    }
140	    for env_name, key in (
141	        ("MOONCAKE_GLOBAL_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
142	        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
143	        ("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
144	        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
145	    ):
146	        if os.environ.get(env_name):
147	            setup_kwargs[key] = int(os.environ[env_name])
148	    return MooncakeFeatureStore(
149	        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
150	        setup_kwargs=setup_kwargs,
151	        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
152	        credential=token,
153	        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
154	    )
155	
156	
157	def _offline_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
158	    backend = os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir")
159	    if backend == "mooncake":
160	        return _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=retain_on_release)
161	    if backend != "shared_dir":
162	        raise ValueError(
163	            f"unknown DISAGG_BACKEND={backend!r}; expected shared_dir or mooncake"
164	        )
165	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import (
166	        AuthPolicy,
167	        SharedDirFeatureStore,
168	    )
169	
170	    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
171	    return SharedDirFeatureStore(
172	        _env("DISAGG_STORE_ROOT"),
173	        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
174	        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
175	        credential=token,
176	        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
177	    )
178	
179	
180	def _server_urls(cfg: Config) -> list[str]:
181	    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
182	    if deployment is not None and deployment.server_urls:
183	        return list(deployment.server_urls)
184	    raw = os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URLS") or os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URL")
185	    if not raw:
186	        raise ValueError(
187	            "online producer requires deployment.disaggregated.server_urls, "
188	            "DISAGG_SERVER_URLS, or DISAGG_SERVER_URL"
189	        )
190	    return [item.strip() for item in raw.split(",") if item.strip()]
191	
192	
193	def _consumer_database_path(cfg: Config) -> Optional[str]:
194	    configured = os.environ.get("DISAGG_DB")
195	    if configured:
196	        return configured
197	    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
198	    if deployment is None:
199	        return None
200	    state_dir = deployment.consumer_state_dir or deployment.control_dir
201	    return os.path.join(state_dir, "consumer.sqlite")
202	
203	
204	def _online_schedule_payload(cfg: Config, *, num_prompts: int) -> dict:
205	    """Describe the exact finite online schedule prepared by the producer."""
206	    from specforge.training.schedule import resolve_online_total_steps
207	
208	    trainer = cfg.deployment.trainer
209	    dp_size = trainer.nnodes * trainer.nproc_per_node
210	    total_steps = resolve_online_total_steps(
211	        num_prompts=num_prompts,
212	        prompt_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
213	        dp_size=dp_size,
214	        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
215	        accumulation_steps=cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
216	    )
217	    return {
218	        "version": 1,
219	        "total_steps": total_steps,
220	        "num_prompts": num_prompts,
221	        "prompt_epochs": cfg.training.num_epochs,
222	        "prompt_seed": cfg.training.seed,
223	        "dp_size": dp_size,
224	        "batch_size": cfg.training.batch_size,
225	        "accumulation_steps": cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
226	    }
227	
228	
229	def _read_online_total_steps(cfg: Config, channel_path: str) -> int:
230	    """Read and validate the producer-owned online schedule contract."""
231	    schedule_path = channel_path + _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX
232	    _wait_for(
233	        schedule_path,
234	        timeout_s=_optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT"),
235	        failure_path=channel_path + ".failed",
236	    )
237	    try:
238	        with open(schedule_path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
239	            payload = json.load(stream)
240	    except (OSError, ValueError) as exc:
241	        raise ValueError(
242	            f"invalid online schedule record {schedule_path}: {exc}"
243	        ) from exc
244	
245	    trainer = cfg.deployment.trainer
246	    expected = {
247	        "version": 1,
248	        "prompt_epochs": cfg.training.num_epochs,
249	        "prompt_seed": cfg.training.seed,
250	        "dp_size": trainer.nnodes * trainer.nproc_per_node,
251	        "batch_size": cfg.training.batch_size,
252	        "accumulation_steps": cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
253	    }
254	    mismatches = {
255	        key: (payload.get(key), value)
256	        for key, value in expected.items()
257	        if payload.get(key) != value
258	    }
259	    if mismatches:
260	        raise ValueError(
261	            f"online schedule record {schedule_path} does not match this "
262	            f"consumer configuration: {mismatches}"
263	        )
264	    total_steps = payload.get("total_steps")
265	    if not isinstance(total_steps, int) or total_steps < 1:
266	        raise ValueError(
267	            f"online schedule record {schedule_path} has invalid "
268	            f"total_steps={total_steps!r}"
269	        )
270	    return total_steps
271	
272	
273	def _optional_timeout_s(name: str) -> Optional[float]:
274	    """Read an explicitly configured positive timeout.
275	
276	    Long-running producer/consumer coordination is unbounded by default.  A
277	    deployment may opt into a terminal timeout through the corresponding
278	    environment variable, but zero and negative values are almost certainly a
279	    configuration mistake and match neither the schema nor useful semantics.
280	    """
281	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
282	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
283	        return None
284	    try:
285	        timeout_s = float(raw)
286	    except ValueError as exc:
287	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a positive number, got {raw!r}") from exc
288	    if timeout_s <= 0:
289	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be positive, got {raw!r}")
290	    return timeout_s
291	
292	
293	def _wait_for(
294	    path: str,
295	    *,
296	    timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
297	    failure_path: Optional[str] = None,
298	) -> None:
299	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s if timeout_s is not None else None
300	    while not os.path.exists(path):
301	        failure = _read_control(failure_path) if failure_path else None
302	        if failure is not None:
303	            raise RuntimeError(
304	                f"remote role failed while waiting for {path}: {failure}"
305	            )
306	        if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
307	            raise TimeoutError(f"timed out waiting for {path}")
308	        time.sleep(0.25)
309	
310	
311	def _hold_mooncake_producer(manifest: str) -> None:
312	    if os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir") != "mooncake":
313	        return
314	    consumed = manifest + ".consumed"
315	    _wait_for(
316	        consumed,
317	        timeout_s=_optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PRODUCER_HOLD_S"),
318	        failure_path=manifest + ".consumer_failed",
319	    )
320	
321	
322	def _cleanup_offline_mooncake_refs(store, refs: Sequence, *, reason: str) -> None:
323	    """Remove every object owned by one terminal offline producer attempt."""
324	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import drain_feature_store_removals
325	
326	    cleanup_errors = []
327	    for ref in refs:
328	        try:
329	            store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=reason)
330	        except Exception as exc:
331	            cleanup_errors.append(f"{ref.sample_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
332	    try:
333	        drain_feature_store_removals(store)
334	    except Exception as exc:
335	        cleanup_errors.append(f"pending-remove drain: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
336	    if cleanup_errors:
337	        raise RuntimeError(
338	            "offline Mooncake cleanup did not remove every ingested sample: "
339	            f"{cleanup_errors}"
340	        )
341	
342	
343	def _build_offline(
344	    cfg: Config,
345	    *,
346	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
347	    build_model_bundle: Callable,
348	    optimizer_factory: Callable,
349	    logger: Callable,
350	):
351	    from specforge.training.assembly import (
352	        TrainingRun,
353	        _dataloader_num_workers,
354	        _profiling_options,
355	    )
356	
357	    manifest = _env("DISAGG_MANIFEST")
358	    done = manifest + ".done"
359	
360	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
361	        _claim_fresh_control_path(manifest, _OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES)
362	
363	        def produce() -> int:
364	            from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disagg_ingest import (
365	                ingest_offline_features,
366	                write_ref_manifest,
367	            )
368	
369	            store = None
370	            tracked_refs = []
371	            produced = 0
372	            primary_exc = None
373	            try:
374	                store = _offline_store(cfg)
375	                refs = ingest_offline_features(
376	                    store,
377	                    cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
378	                    algorithm_name=algorithm.name,
379	                    build_reader=algorithm.providers.offline_for(
380	                        cfg.model.input_modality
381	                    ).build_reader,
382	                    run_id=cfg.run_id,
383	                    ttt_length=cfg.training.ttt_length,
384	                    max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
385	                    on_ref=tracked_refs.append,
386	                )
387	                produced = len(refs)
388	                write_ref_manifest(refs, manifest)
389	                _write_control(done)
390	                _hold_mooncake_producer(manifest)
391	            except BaseException as exc:
392	                primary_exc = exc
393	                # Publish the failure sentinel BEFORE the Mooncake abort sweep:
394	                # the sweep can take minutes over thousands of refs, and a
395	                # supervisor SIGKILL at grace expiry mid-sweep must not leave a
396	                # remote consumer waiting forever on a sentinel that never
397	                # arrives. A cleanup failure below overwrites this record with
398	                # the combined error.
399	                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", primary_exc)
400	
401	            cleanup_exc = None
402	            if (
403	                store is not None
404	                and os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir") == "mooncake"
405	            ):
406	                try:
407	                    _cleanup_offline_mooncake_refs(
408	                        store,
409	                        tracked_refs,
410	                        reason=(
411	                            "offline-attempt-failed"
412	                            if primary_exc is not None
413	                            else "offline-attempt-finished"
414	                        ),
415	                    )
416	                except Exception as exc:
417	                    cleanup_exc = exc
418	
419	            if primary_exc is not None and cleanup_exc is not None:
420	                combined = RuntimeError(
421	                    f"offline producer failed ({type(primary_exc).__name__}: "
422	                    f"{primary_exc}) and Mooncake cleanup also failed "
423	                    f"({type(cleanup_exc).__name__}: {cleanup_exc})"
424	                )
425	                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", combined)
426	                raise combined from primary_exc
427	            if primary_exc is not None:
428	                raise primary_exc
429	            if cleanup_exc is not None:
430	                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", cleanup_exc)
431	                raise cleanup_exc
432	            return produced
433	
434	        return TrainingRun(execute=produce)
435	
436	    _wait_for(done, failure_path=manifest + ".failed")
437	    from specforge.launch import build_disagg_offline_runtime
438	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disagg_ingest import read_ref_manifest
439	
440	    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg)
441	    accumulation_steps = cfg.training.accumulation_steps
442	    if cfg.training.attention_backend == "usp":
443	        accumulation_steps *= cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size * cfg.training.sp_ring_size
444	    trainer = build_disagg_offline_runtime(
445	        algorithm=algorithm,
446	        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
447	        feature_store=_offline_store(cfg, retain_on_release=True),
448	        refs=read_ref_manifest(manifest),
449	        draft_model=bundle.model,
450	        target_head=bundle.target_head,
451	        optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory(cfg),
452	        run_id=cfg.run_id,
453	        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
454	        ttt_length=cfg.training.ttt_length,
455	        max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
456	        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
457	        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
458	        num_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
459	        max_steps=cfg.training.max_steps,
460	        total_steps=cfg.training.total_steps,
461	        save_interval=cfg.training.save_interval,
462	        eval_interval=cfg.training.eval_interval,
463	        eval_hidden_states_path=cfg.data.eval_hidden_states_path or None,
464	        logger=logger,
465	        log_interval=cfg.training.log_interval,
466	        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
467	        resume_from=cfg.training.resume_from,
468	        max_checkpoints=cfg.training.max_checkpoints,
469	        tp_size=cfg.training.tp_size,
470	        sp_ulysses_size=cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size,
471	        sp_ring_size=cfg.training.sp_ring_size,
472	        use_usp_preprocess=(cfg.training.attention_backend == "usp"),
473	        seed=cfg.training.seed,
474	        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
475	        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
476	    )
477	
478	    def mark_consumed(_step: int) -> None:
479	        if _primary_rank():
480	            _write_control(manifest + ".consumed")
481	
482	    def mark_consumer_failed(exc: BaseException) -> None:
483	        _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".consumer_failed", exc)
484	
485	    return TrainingRun(
486	        trainer=trainer,
487	        on_success=mark_consumed,
488	        on_failure=mark_consumer_failed,
489	    )

492	def _producer_capture_metadata(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration):
493	    from specforge.training.capture_contract import resolve_server_capture_contract
494	
495	    contract = resolve_server_capture_contract(cfg, algorithm=algorithm)
496	    return (
497	        list(contract.aux_layer_ids),
498	        contract.target_hidden_size,
499	        contract.target_vocab_size,
500	        contract.draft_vocab_size,
501	    )

504	def _build_online(
505	    cfg: Config,
506	    *,
507	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
508	    build_model_bundle: Callable,
509	    prepare_prompts: Callable,
510	    optimizer_factory: Callable,
511	    logger: Callable,
512	):
513	    from specforge.training.assembly import (
514	        TrainingRun,
515	        _dataloader_num_workers,
516	        _load_input_tools,
517	        _profiling_options,
518	    )
519	
520	    modality = cfg.model.input_modality
521	    streaming = algorithm.providers.server_streaming_for(modality)
522	    channel_path = _env("DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL")
523	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
524	        _claim_fresh_control_path(channel_path, _ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES)
525	    # The producer owns capture and explicit attempt cleanup. The consumer must
526	    # retain materialized features until DPAckController commits the optimizer
527	    # boundary and explicitly aborts the acknowledged ids.
528	    store = _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=cfg.training.role == "consumer")
529	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import drain_feature_store_removals
530	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.streaming_ref_channel import StreamingRefChannel
531	
532	    channel = StreamingRefChannel(channel_path)
533	
534	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
535	        from specforge.inference.adapters.server_capture import (
536	            ServerCaptureSchema,
537	            SGLangServerCaptureAdapter,
538	        )
539	        from specforge.launch import build_disagg_online_producer
540	        from specforge.training.model_loading import resolve_draft_config
541	
542	        input_adapter = streaming.create_input_adapter(cfg)
543	        input_tools = _load_input_tools(
544	            cfg,
545	            algorithm,
546	            input_adapter=input_adapter,
547	        )
548	        draft_config = resolve_draft_config(
549	            cfg,
550	            provider=algorithm.providers.model.draft_config,
551	        )
552	        if input_adapter is None:
553	            prompts = prepare_prompts(
554	                cfg,
555	                input_tools,
556	                draft_config=draft_config,
557	            )
558	        else:
559	            prompts = input_adapter.prepare_prompts(
560	                cfg,
561	                input_tools,
562	                draft_config=draft_config,
563	            )
564	        if cfg.training.total_steps is None and cfg.training.max_steps is None:
565	            schedule = _online_schedule_payload(cfg, num_prompts=len(prompts))
566	            _write_control(
567	                channel_path + _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
568	                json.dumps(schedule, sort_keys=True),
569	            )
570	        layers, hidden_size, target_vocab, draft_vocab = _producer_capture_metadata(
571	            cfg, algorithm
572	        )
573	        layout = streaming.layout
574	        capture_schema = ServerCaptureSchema(
575	            aux_feature=layout.aux_feature,
576	            last_hidden_feature=layout.last_hidden_feature,
577	            passthrough=layout.passthrough,
578	            attention_mask_feature=layout.attention_mask_feature,
579	        )
580	        adapters = [
581	            SGLangServerCaptureAdapter(
582	                url,
583	                store,
584	                run_id=cfg.run_id,
585	                algorithm=algorithm.name,
586	                schema=capture_schema,
587	                request_input_adapter=input_adapter,
588	                target_model_version=cfg.model.target_model_path,
589	            )
590	            for url in _server_urls(cfg)
591	        ]
592	        target_repr = streaming.target_representation
593	        peer_wait_timeout_s = _optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT")
594	        high_watermark_override = os.environ.get("DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK")
595	        in_flight_high_watermark = int(
596	            high_watermark_override or cfg.runtime.in_flight_high_watermark
597	        )
598	        low_watermark_override = os.environ.get("DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_LOW_WATERMARK")
599	        # Preserve the legacy one-watermark environment override: when only the
600	        # old high value is supplied, resume at that same threshold.
601	        in_flight_low_watermark = (
602	            int(low_watermark_override)
603	            if low_watermark_override is not None
604	            else (
605	                None
606	                if high_watermark_override is not None
607	                else cfg.runtime.in_flight_low_watermark
608	            )
609	        )
610	        _workers, drive = build_disagg_online_producer(
611	            algorithm=algorithm,
612	            modality=modality,
613	            prompts=prompts,
614	            feature_store=store,
615	            channel=channel,
616	            feature_source=adapters if len(adapters) > 1 else adapters[0],
617	            num_rollout_workers=len(adapters),
618	            run_id=cfg.run_id,
619	            target_hidden_size=hidden_size,
620	            target_vocab_size=target_vocab,
621	            draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab,
622	            target_repr=target_repr,
623	            aux_hidden_state_layer_ids=layers,
624	            prompt_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
625	            prompt_seed=cfg.training.seed,
626	            lease=cfg.runtime.producer_lease,
627	            in_flight_high_watermark=in_flight_high_watermark,
628	            in_flight_low_watermark=in_flight_low_watermark,
629	            resident_high_watermark_bytes=(
630	                int(os.environ["DISAGG_RESIDENT_HIGH_WATERMARK_BYTES"])
631	                if os.environ.get("DISAGG_RESIDENT_HIGH_WATERMARK_BYTES")
632	                else cfg.runtime.resident_high_watermark_bytes
633	            ),
634	            resident_low_watermark_bytes=(
635	                int(os.environ["DISAGG_RESIDENT_LOW_WATERMARK_BYTES"])
636	                if os.environ.get("DISAGG_RESIDENT_LOW_WATERMARK_BYTES")
637	                else cfg.runtime.resident_low_watermark_bytes
638	            ),
639	            feature_store_max_resident_bytes=(
640	                cfg.runtime.feature_store_max_resident_bytes
641	            ),
642	            peer_wait_timeout_s=peer_wait_timeout_s,
643	        )
644	
645	        def produce() -> int:
646	            produced = 0
647	            primary_exc = None
648	            try:
649	                produced = drive(should_stop=channel.consumer_stopped)
650	                deadline = (
651	                    time.monotonic() + peer_wait_timeout_s
652	                    if peer_wait_timeout_s is not None
653	                    else None
654	                )
655	                while not channel.consumer_stopped():
656	                    if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
657	                        raise TimeoutError(
658	                            "producer timed out waiting for the consumer result "
659	                            f"after {peer_wait_timeout_s:.0f}s"
660	                        )
661	                    time.sleep(0.25)
662	                consumer_failure = channel.consumer_failure()
663	                if consumer_failure is not None:
664	                    raise RuntimeError(f"consumer failed: {consumer_failure}")
665	            except BaseException as exc:
666	                primary_exc = exc
667	                try:
668	                    channel.fail(f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
669	                except Exception as signal_exc:
670	                    print(
671	                        f"failed to publish producer failure: {signal_exc}",
672	                        flush=True,
673	                    )
674	            cleanup_errors = []
675	            try:
676	                reader = StreamingRefChannel(channel_path)
677	                while True:
678	                    refs = reader.poll(max_n=1024)
679	                    if not refs:
680	                        break
681	                    for ref in refs:
682	                        try:
683	                            store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason="online-attempt-finished")
684	                        except Exception as exc:
685	                            cleanup_errors.append(
686	                                f"{ref.sample_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
687	                            )
688	            except Exception as exc:
689	                cleanup_errors.append(
690	                    f"published-ref scan: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
691	                )
692	            try:
693	                store.discard_external_attempts(
694	                    reason="online-attempt-unadopted-capture"
695	                )
696	            except Exception as exc:
697	                cleanup_errors.append(
698	                    f"unadopted-capture cleanup: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
699	                )
700	            try:
701	                drain_feature_store_removals(store)
702	            except Exception as exc:
703	                cleanup_errors.append(
704	                    f"pending-remove drain: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
705	                )
706	            if primary_exc is not None and cleanup_errors:
707	                raise RuntimeError(
708	                    f"producer failed ({type(primary_exc).__name__}: "
709	                    f"{primary_exc}) and Mooncake cleanup also failed: "
710	                    f"{cleanup_errors}"
711	                ) from primary_exc
712	            if primary_exc is not None:
713	                raise primary_exc
714	            if cleanup_errors:
715	                raise RuntimeError(
716	                    "producer could not clean all published Mooncake features: "
717	                    f"{cleanup_errors}"
718	                )
719	            return produced
720	
721	        return TrainingRun(execute=produce)
722	
723	    from specforge.launch import build_disagg_online_consumer
724	
725	    total_steps = cfg.training.total_steps
726	    if total_steps is None and cfg.training.max_steps is None:
727	        total_steps = _read_online_total_steps(cfg, channel_path)
728	    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg)
729	    trainer = build_disagg_online_consumer(
730	        algorithm=algorithm,
731	        modality=modality,
732	        feature_store=store,
733	        channel=channel,
734	        draft_model=bundle.model,
735	        target_head=bundle.target_head,
736	        optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory(cfg),
737	        run_id=cfg.run_id,
738	        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
739	        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
740	        accumulation_steps=cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
741	        max_steps=cfg.training.max_steps,
742	        total_steps=total_steps,
743	        save_interval=cfg.training.save_interval,
744	        idle_timeout_s=float(os.environ.get("DISAGG_IDLE_TIMEOUT", "0")) or None,
745	        metadata_db_path=_consumer_database_path(cfg),
746	        logger=logger,
747	        log_interval=cfg.training.log_interval,
748	        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
749	        max_checkpoints=cfg.training.max_checkpoints,
750	        tp_size=cfg.training.tp_size,
751	        sp_ulysses_size=cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size,
752	        sp_ring_size=cfg.training.sp_ring_size,
753	        inbox_dir=os.environ.get("DISAGG_INBOX_DIR") or None,
754	        resume_from=cfg.training.resume_from,
755	        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
756	        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
757	    )
758	
759	    return TrainingRun(trainer=trainer)
760	
761	
762	def build_disaggregated_run(
763	    cfg: Config,
764	    *,
765	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
766	    build_model_bundle: Callable,
767	    prepare_prompts: Callable,
768	    optimizer_factory: Callable,
769	    logger: Callable,
770	):
771	    """Assemble the configured producer or consumer role."""
772	    if cfg.training.role not in ("producer", "consumer"):
773	        raise ValueError(
774	            "disaggregated runs require training.role=producer or consumer"
775	        )
776	    try:
777	        if cfg.mode == "offline":
778	            return _build_offline(
779	                cfg,
780	                algorithm=algorithm,
781	                build_model_bundle=build_model_bundle,
782	                optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory,
783	                logger=logger,
784	            )
785	        return _build_online(
786	            cfg,
787	            algorithm=algorithm,
788	            build_model_bundle=build_model_bundle,
789	            prepare_prompts=prepare_prompts,
790	            optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory,
791	            logger=logger,
792	        )
793	    except BaseException as exc:
794	        _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg, exc)
795	        raise
796	
797	
798	__all__ = ["build_disaggregated_run"]
